aggregations.description:
Sub-aggregations for this aggregation.
Only applies to bucket aggregations.

adjacency_matrix.description:
A bucket aggregation returning a form of adjacency matrix.
The request provides a collection of named filter expressions, similar to the `filters` aggregation.
Each bucket in the response represents a non-empty cell in the matrix of intersecting filters.

auto_date_histogram.description: A multi-bucket aggregation similar to the date histogram, except instead of providing an interval to use as the width of each bucket, a target number of buckets is provided.

avg.description: A single-value metrics aggregation that computes the average of numeric values that are extracted from the aggregated documents.

avg_bucket.description:
A sibling pipeline aggregation which calculates the mean value of a specified metric in a sibling aggregation.
The specified metric must be numeric and the sibling aggregation must be a multi-bucket aggregation.

boxplot.description: A metrics aggregation that computes a box plot of numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

bucket_script.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which runs a script which can perform per bucket computations on metrics in the parent multi-bucket aggregation.

bucket_selector.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which runs a script to determine whether the current bucket will be retained in the parent multi-bucket aggregation.

bucket_sort.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which sorts the buckets of its parent multi-bucket aggregation.

bucket_count_ks_test.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which runs a two sample Kolmogorov–Smirnov test ("K-S test") against a provided distribution and the distribution implied by the documents counts in the configured sibling aggregation.

bucket_correlation.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which runs a correlation function on the configured sibling multi-bucket aggregation.

cardinality.description: A single-value metrics aggregation that calculates an approximate count of distinct values.

cartesian_bounds.description: A metric aggregation that computes the spatial bounding box containing all values for a Point or Shape field.

cartesian_centroid.description: A metric aggregation that computes the weighted centroid from all coordinate values for point and shape fields.

categorize_text.description: A multi-bucket aggregation that groups semi-structured text into buckets.

change_point.description:
A sibling pipeline that detects, spikes, dips, and change points in a metric.
Given a distribution of values provided by the sibling multi-bucket aggregation,
this aggregation indicates the bucket of any spike or dip and/or the bucket at which
the largest change in the distribution of values, if they are statistically significant.
There must be at least 22 bucketed values. Fewer than 1,000 is preferred.

children.description: A single bucket aggregation that selects child documents that have the specified type, as defined in a `join` field.

composite.description:
A multi-bucket aggregation that creates composite buckets from different sources.
Unlike the other multi-bucket aggregations, you can use the `composite` aggregation to paginate *all* buckets from a multi-level aggregation efficiently.

cumulative_cardinality.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which calculates the cumulative cardinality in a parent `histogram` or `date_histogram` aggregation.

cumulative_sum.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which calculates the cumulative sum of a specified metric in a parent `histogram` or `date_histogram` aggregation.

date_histogram.description:
A multi-bucket values source based aggregation that can be applied on date values or date range values extracted from the documents.
It dynamically builds fixed size (interval) buckets over the values.

date_range.description: A multi-bucket value source based aggregation that enables the user to define a set of date ranges - each representing a bucket.

derivative.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which calculates the derivative of a specified metric in a parent `histogram` or `date_histogram` aggregation.

diversified_sampler.description:
A filtering aggregation used to limit any sub aggregations' processing to a sample of the top-scoring documents.
Similar to the `sampler` aggregation, but adds the ability to limit the number of matches that share a common value.

extended_stats.description: A multi-value metrics aggregation that computes stats over numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

extended_stats_bucket.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which calculates a variety of stats across all bucket of a specified metric in a sibling aggregation.

frequent_item_sets.description: A bucket aggregation which finds frequent item sets, a form of association rules mining that identifies items that often occur together.

filter.description: A single bucket aggregation that narrows the set of documents to those that match a query.

filters.description: A multi-bucket aggregation where each bucket contains the documents that match a query.

geo_bounds.description: A metric aggregation that computes the geographic bounding box containing all values for a Geopoint or Geoshape field.

geo_centroid.description: A metric aggregation that computes the weighted centroid from all coordinate values for geo fields.

geo_distance.description:
A multi-bucket aggregation that works on `geo_point` fields.
Evaluates the distance of each document value from an origin point and determines the buckets it belongs to, based on ranges defined in the request.

geohash_grid.description:
A multi-bucket aggregation that groups `geo_point` and `geo_shape` values into buckets that represent a grid.
Each cell is labeled using a geohash which is of user-definable precision.

geo_line.description: Aggregates all `geo_point` values within a bucket into a `LineString` ordered by the chosen sort field.

geotile_grid.description:
A multi-bucket aggregation that groups `geo_point` and `geo_shape` values into buckets that represent a grid.
Each cell corresponds to a map tile as used by many online map sites.

geohex_grid.description:
A multi-bucket aggregation that groups `geo_point` and `geo_shape` values into buckets that represent a grid.
Each cell corresponds to a H3 cell index and is labeled using the H3Index representation.

global.description:
Defines a single bucket of all the documents within the search execution context.
This context is defined by the indices and the document types you’re searching on, but is not influenced by the search query itself.

histogram.description:
A multi-bucket values source based aggregation that can be applied on numeric values or numeric range values extracted from the documents.
It dynamically builds fixed size (interval) buckets over the values.

ip_range.description: A multi-bucket value source based aggregation that enables the user to define a set of IP ranges - each representing a bucket.

ip_prefix.description: A bucket aggregation that groups documents based on the network or sub-network of an IP address.

inference.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which loads a pre-trained model and performs inference on the collated result fields from the parent bucket aggregation.

matrix_stats.description: A numeric aggregation that computes the following statistics over a set of document fields: `count`, `mean`, `variance`, `skewness`, `kurtosis`, `covariance`, and `covariance`.

max.description: A single-value metrics aggregation that returns the maximum value among the numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

max_bucket.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which identifies the bucket(s) with the maximum value of a specified metric in a sibling aggregation and outputs both the value and the key(s) of the bucket(s).

median_absolute_deviation.description: A single-value aggregation that approximates the median absolute deviation of its search results.

min.description: A single-value metrics aggregation that returns the minimum value among numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

min_bucket.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which identifies the bucket(s) with the minimum value of a specified metric in a sibling aggregation and outputs both the value and the key(s) of the bucket(s).

missing.description: A field data based single bucket aggregation, that creates a bucket of all documents in the current document set context that are missing a field value (effectively, missing a field or having the configured NULL value set).

moving_percentiles.description: Given an ordered series of percentiles, "slides" a window across those percentiles and computes cumulative percentiles.

moving_fn.description:
Given an ordered series of data, "slides" a window across the data and runs a custom script on each window of data.
For convenience, a number of common functions are predefined such as `min`, `max`, and moving averages.

multi_terms.description: A multi-bucket value source based aggregation where buckets are dynamically built - one per unique set of values.

nested.description: A special single bucket aggregation that enables aggregating nested documents.

normalize.description: A parent pipeline aggregation which calculates the specific normalized/rescaled value for a specific bucket value.

parent.description: A special single bucket aggregation that selects parent documents that have the specified type, as defined in a `join` field.

percentile_ranks.description: A multi-value metrics aggregation that calculates one or more percentile ranks over numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

percentiles.description: A multi-value metrics aggregation that calculates one or more percentiles over numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

percentiles_bucket.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which calculates percentiles across all bucket of a specified metric in a sibling aggregation.

range.description: A multi-bucket value source based aggregation that enables the user to define a set of ranges - each representing a bucket.

rare_terms.description: A multi-bucket value source based aggregation which finds "rare" terms — terms that are at the long-tail of the distribution and are not frequent.

rate.description:
Calculates a rate of documents or a field in each bucket.
Can only be used inside a `date_histogram` or `composite` aggregation.

reverse_nested.description:
A special single bucket aggregation that enables aggregating on parent documents from nested documents.
Should only be defined inside a `nested` aggregation.

sampler.description: A filtering aggregation used to limit any sub aggregations' processing to a sample of the top-scoring documents.

scripted_metric.description: A metric aggregation that uses scripts to provide a metric output.

serial_diff.description: An aggregation that subtracts values in a time series from themselves at different time lags or periods.

significant_terms.description: Returns interesting or unusual occurrences of terms in a set.

significant_text.description: Returns interesting or unusual occurrences of free-text terms in a set.

stats.description: A multi-value metrics aggregation that computes stats over numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

stats_bucket.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which calculates a variety of stats across all bucket of a specified metric in a sibling aggregation.

string_stats.description: A multi-value metrics aggregation that computes statistics over string values extracted from the aggregated documents.

sum.description: A single-value metrics aggregation that sums numeric values that are extracted from the aggregated documents.

sum_bucket.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which calculates the sum of a specified metric across all buckets in a sibling aggregation.

terms.description: A multi-bucket value source based aggregation where buckets are dynamically built - one per unique value.

time_series.description:
The time series aggregation queries data created using a time series index.
This is typically data such as metrics or other data streams with a time component, and requires creating an index using the time series mode.

top_hits.description: A metric aggregation that returns the top matching documents per bucket.

t_test.description: A metrics aggregation that performs a statistical hypothesis test in which the test statistic follows a Student’s t-distribution under the null hypothesis on numeric values extracted from the aggregated documents.

top_metrics.description: A metric aggregation that selects metrics from the document with the largest or smallest sort value.

value_count.description: A single-value metrics aggregation that counts the number of values that are extracted from the aggregated documents.

weighted_avg.description: A single-value metrics aggregation that computes the weighted average of numeric values that are extracted from the aggregated documents.

variable_width_histogram.description: A multi-bucket aggregation similar to the histogram, except instead of providing an interval to use as the width of each bucket, a target number of buckets is provided.

filters.description:
Filters used to create buckets.
At least one filter is required.

separator.description: Separator used to concatenate filter names. Defaults to &.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

format.description:
The date format used to format `key_as_string` in the response.
If no `format` is specified, the first date format specified in the field mapping is used.

minimum_interval.description:
The minimum rounding interval.
This can make the collection process more efficient, as the aggregation will not attempt to round at any interval lower than `minimum_interval`.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

offset.description: Time zone specified as a ISO 8601 UTC offset.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

_types.aggregations__BucketsPath.description:
Buckets path can be expressed in different ways, and an aggregation may accept some or all of these
forms depending on its type. Please refer to each aggregation's documentation to know what buckets
path forms they accept.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

compression.description: Limits the maximum number of nodes used by the underlying TDigest algorithm to `20 * compression`, enabling control of memory usage and approximation error.

execution_hint.description:
The default implementation of TDigest is optimized for performance, scaling to millions or even billions of sample values while maintaining acceptable accuracy levels (close to 1% relative error for millions of samples in some cases).
To use an implementation optimized for accuracy, set this parameter to high_accuracy instead.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

script.description: The script to run for this aggregation.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

script.description: The script to run for this aggregation.

from.description: Buckets in positions prior to `from` will be truncated.

gap_policy.description: The policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

size.description:
The number of buckets to return.
Defaults to all buckets of the parent aggregation.

sort.description: The list of fields to sort on.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

alternative.description:
A list of string values indicating which K-S test alternative to calculate. The valid values
are: "greater", "less", "two_sided". This parameter is key for determining the K-S statistic used
when calculating the K-S test. Default value is all possible alternative hypotheses.

fractions.description:
A list of doubles indicating the distribution of the samples with which to compare to the `buckets_path` results.
In typical usage this is the overall proportion of documents in each bucket, which is compared with the actual
document proportions in each bucket from the sibling aggregation counts. The default is to assume that overall
documents are uniformly distributed on these buckets, which they would be if one used equal percentiles of a
metric to define the bucket end points.

sampling_method.description:
Indicates the sampling methodology when calculating the K-S test. Note, this is sampling of the returned values.
This determines the cumulative distribution function (CDF) points used comparing the two samples. Default is
`upper_tail`, which emphasizes the upper end of the CDF points. Valid options are: `upper_tail`, `uniform`,
and `lower_tail`.

_types.aggregations__BucketKsAggregation.description:
A sibling pipeline aggregation which executes a two sample Kolmogorov–Smirnov test (referred
to as a "K-S test" from now on) against a provided distribution, and the distribution implied
by the documents counts in the configured sibling aggregation. Specifically, for some metric,
assuming that the percentile intervals of the metric are known beforehand or have been computed
by an aggregation, then one would use range aggregation for the sibling to compute the p-value
of the distribution difference between the metric and the restriction of that metric to a subset
of the documents. A natural use case is if the sibling aggregation range aggregation nested in a
terms aggregation, in which case one compares the overall distribution of metric to its restriction
to each term.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

_types.aggregations__BucketCorrelationAg.description: A sibling pipeline aggregation which executes a correlation function on the configured sibling multi-bucket aggregation.

count_correlation.description: The configuration to calculate a count correlation. This function is designed for determining the correlation of a term value and a given metric.

indicator.description: The indicator with which to correlate the configured `bucket_path` values.

doc_count.description:
The total number of documents that initially created the expectations. It’s required to be greater
than or equal to the sum of all values in the buckets_path as this is the originating superset of data
to which the term values are correlated.

expectations.description:
An array of numbers with which to correlate the configured `bucket_path` values.
The length of this value must always equal the number of buckets returned by the `bucket_path`.

fractions.description:
An array of fractions to use when averaging and calculating variance. This should be used if
the pre-calculated data and the buckets_path have known gaps. The length of fractions, if provided,
must equal expectations.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

precision_threshold.description:
A unique count below which counts are expected to be close to accurate.
This allows to trade memory for accuracy.

execution_hint.description: Mechanism by which cardinality aggregations is run.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

field.description: The semi-structured text field to categorize.

max_unique_tokens.description:
The maximum number of unique tokens at any position up to max_matched_tokens. Must be larger than 1.
Smaller values use less memory and create fewer categories. Larger values will use more memory and
create narrower categories. Max allowed value is 100.

max_matched_tokens.description:
The maximum number of token positions to match on before attempting to merge categories. Larger
values will use more memory and create narrower categories. Max allowed value is 100.

similarity_threshold.description:
The minimum percentage of tokens that must match for text to be added to the category bucket. Must
be between 1 and 100. The larger the value the narrower the categories. Larger values will increase memory
usage and create narrower categories.

categorization_filters.description:
This property expects an array of regular expressions. The expressions are used to filter out matching
sequences from the categorization field values. You can use this functionality to fine tune the categorization
by excluding sequences from consideration when categories are defined. For example, you can exclude SQL
statements that appear in your log files. This property cannot be used at the same time as categorization_analyzer.
If you only want to define simple regular expression filters that are applied prior to tokenization, setting
this property is the easiest method. If you also want to customize the tokenizer or post-tokenization filtering,
use the categorization_analyzer property instead and include the filters as pattern_replace character filters.

categorization_analyzer.description:
The categorization analyzer specifies how the text is analyzed and tokenized before being categorized.
The syntax is very similar to that used to define the analyzer in the analyze API. This property
cannot be used at the same time as `categorization_filters`.

shard_size.description: The number of categorization buckets to return from each shard before merging all the results.

size.description: The number of buckets to return.

min_doc_count.description: The minimum number of documents in a bucket to be returned to the results.

shard_min_doc_count.description: The minimum number of documents in a bucket to be returned from the shard before merging.

_types.aggregations__CategorizeTextAggre.description:
A multi-bucket aggregation that groups semi-structured text into buckets. Each text
field is re-analyzed using a custom analyzer. The resulting tokens are then categorized
creating buckets of similarly formatted text values. This aggregation works best with machine
generated text like system logs. Only the first 100 analyzed tokens are used to categorize the text.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

type.description: The child type that should be selected.

after.description: When paginating, use the `after_key` value returned in the previous response to retrieve the next page.

size.description: The number of composite buckets that should be returned.

sources.description:
The value sources used to build composite buckets.
Keys are returned in the order of the `sources` definition.

field.description: Either `field` or `script` must be present
Either `field` or `script` must be present
Either `field` or `script` must be present
Either `field` or `script` must be present

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

calendar_interval.description:
Calendar-aware interval.
Can be specified using the unit name, such as `month`, or as a single unit quantity, such as `1M`.

extended_bounds.description: Enables extending the bounds of the histogram beyond the data itself.

hard_bounds.description: Limits the histogram to specified bounds.

field.description: The date field whose values are use to build a histogram.

fixed_interval.description: Fixed intervals: a fixed number of SI units and never deviate, regardless of where they fall on the calendar.

format.description:
The date format used to format `key_as_string` in the response.
If no `format` is specified, the first date format specified in the field mapping is used.

min_doc_count.description:
Only returns buckets that have `min_doc_count` number of documents.
By default, all buckets between the first bucket that matches documents and the last one are returned.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

offset.description: Changes the start value of each bucket by the specified positive (`+`) or negative offset (`-`) duration.

order.description: The sort order of the returned buckets.

time_zone.description:
Time zone used for bucketing and rounding.
Defaults to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

keyed.description: Set to `true` to associate a unique string key with each bucket and return the ranges as a hash rather than an array.

field.description: The date field whose values are use to build ranges.

format.description: The date format used to format `from` and `to` in the response.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

time_zone.description: Time zone used to convert dates from another time zone to UTC.

keyed.description: Set to `true` to associate a unique string key with each bucket and returns the ranges as a hash rather than an array.

key.description: Custom key to return the range with.

_types.aggregations__FieldDateMath.description:
A date range limit, represented either as a DateMath expression or a number expressed
according to the target field's precision.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

execution_hint.description: The type of value used for de-duplication.

max_docs_per_value.description: Limits how many documents are permitted per choice of de-duplicating value.

shard_size.description: Limits how many top-scoring documents are collected in the sample processed on each shard.

field.description: The field used to provide values used for de-duplication.
The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

sigma.description: The number of standard deviations above/below the mean to display.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

sigma.description: The number of standard deviations above/below the mean to display.

minimum_set_size.description: The minimum size of one item set.

minimum_support.description: The minimum support of one item set.

size.description: The number of top item sets to return.

filter.description: Query that filters documents from analysis.

field.description: Path to field or array of paths. Some API's support wildcards in the path to select multiple fields.

exclude.description:
Values to exclude.
Can be regular expression strings or arrays of strings of exact terms.

include.description:
Values to include.
Can be regular expression strings or arrays of strings of exact terms.

partition.description: The partition number for this request.

filters.description: Collection of queries from which to build buckets.

other_bucket.description: Set to `true` to add a bucket to the response which will contain all documents that do not match any of the given filters.

other_bucket_key.description: The key with which the other bucket is returned.

keyed.description:
By default, the named filters aggregation returns the buckets as an object.
Set to `false` to return the buckets as an array of objects.

_types.aggregations__Buckets.description:
Aggregation buckets. By default they are returned as an array, but if the aggregation has keys configured for
the different buckets, the result is a dictionary.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

wrap_longitude.description: Specifies whether the bounding box should be allowed to overlap the international date line.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

field.description: A field of type `geo_point` used to evaluate the distance.

origin.description: The origin  used to evaluate the distance.

ranges.description: An array of ranges used to bucket documents.

key.description: Custom key to return the range with.

bounds.description: The bounding box to filter the points in each bucket.

field.description:
Field containing indexed `geo_point` or `geo_shape` values.
If the field contains an array, `geohash_grid` aggregates all array values.

precision.description: The string length of the geohashes used to define cells/buckets in the results.

shard_size.description:
Allows for more accurate counting of the top cells returned in the final result the aggregation.
Defaults to returning `max(10,(size x number-of-shards))` buckets from each shard.

size.description: The maximum number of geohash buckets to return.

point.description: The name of the geo_point field.

sort.description:
The name of the numeric field to use as the sort key for ordering the points.
When the `geo_line` aggregation is nested inside a `time_series` aggregation, this field defaults to `@timestamp`, and any other value will result in error.

include_sort.description: When `true`, returns an additional array of the sort values in the feature properties.

sort_order.description: The order in which the line is sorted (ascending or descending).

size.description:
The maximum length of the line represented in the aggregation.
Valid sizes are between 1 and 10000.

field.description: The name of the geo_point field.
The name of the numeric field to use as the sort key for ordering the points.

Field containing indexed `geo_point` or `geo_shape` values.
If the field contains an array, `geotile_grid` aggregates all array values.

precision.description:
Integer zoom of the key used to define cells/buckets in the results.
Values outside of the range [0,29] will be rejected.

shard_size.description:
Allows for more accurate counting of the top cells returned in the final result the aggregation.
Defaults to returning `max(10,(size x number-of-shards))` buckets from each shard.

size.description: The maximum number of buckets to return.

bounds.description: A bounding box to filter the geo-points or geo-shapes in each bucket.

field.description:
Field containing indexed `geo_point` or `geo_shape` values.
If the field contains an array, `geohex_grid` aggregates all array values.

precision.description:
Integer zoom of the key used to defined cells or buckets
in the results. Value should be between 0-15.

bounds.description: Bounding box used to filter the geo-points in each bucket.

size.description: Maximum number of buckets to return.

shard_size.description: Number of buckets returned from each shard.

extended_bounds.description: Enables extending the bounds of the histogram beyond the data itself.

hard_bounds.description:
Limits the range of buckets in the histogram.
It is particularly useful in the case of open data ranges that can result in a very large number of buckets.

field.description: The name of the field to aggregate on.

interval.description:
The interval for the buckets.
Must be a positive decimal.

min_doc_count.description:
Only returns buckets that have `min_doc_count` number of documents.
By default, the response will fill gaps in the histogram with empty buckets.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

offset.description:
By default, the bucket keys start with 0 and then continue in even spaced steps of `interval`.
The bucket boundaries can be shifted by using the `offset` option.

order.description:
The sort order of the returned buckets.
By default, the returned buckets are sorted by their key ascending.

keyed.description: If `true`, returns buckets as a hash instead of an array, keyed by the bucket keys.

field.description: The date field whose values are used to build ranges.

mask.description: IP range defined as a CIDR mask.

field.description: The IP address field to aggregation on. The field mapping type must be `ip`.

prefix_length.description:
Length of the network prefix. For IPv4 addresses the accepted range is [0, 32].
For IPv6 addresses the accepted range is [0, 128].

is_ipv6.description: Defines whether the prefix applies to IPv6 addresses.

append_prefix_length.description: Defines whether the prefix length is appended to IP address keys in the response.

keyed.description: Defines whether buckets are returned as a hash rather than an array in the response.

min_doc_count.description: Minimum number of documents in a bucket for it to be included in the response.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.

compression.description: Limits the maximum number of nodes used by the underlying TDigest algorithm to `20 * compression`, enabling control of memory usage and approximation error.

execution_hint.description:
The default implementation of TDigest is optimized for performance, scaling to millions or even billions of sample values while maintaining acceptable accuracy levels (close to 1% relative error for millions of samples in some cases).
To use an implementation optimized for accuracy, set this parameter to high_accuracy instead.

By default, the window consists of the last n values excluding the current bucket.
Increasing `shift` by 1, moves the starting window position by 1 to the right.

shift.description:
By default, the window consists of the last n values excluding the current bucket.

window.description: The size of window to "slide" across the histogram.

collect_mode.description: Specifies the strategy for data collection.

order.description:
Specifies the sort order of the buckets.
Defaults to sorting by descending document count.

min_doc_count.description: The minimum number of documents in a bucket for it to be returned.

shard_min_doc_count.description: The minimum number of documents in a bucket on each shard for it to be returned.

shard_size.description:
The number of candidate terms produced by each shard.
By default, `shard_size` will be automatically estimated based on the number of shards and the `size` parameter.

show_term_doc_count_error.description: Calculates the doc count error on per term basis.

size.description: The number of term buckets should be returned out of the overall terms list.

terms.description: The field from which to generate sets of terms.

field.description:
A field from which to retrieve terms.
It is required if `script` is not provided.

script.description:
A script to calculate terms to aggregate on.
It is required if `field` is not provided.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

path.description: The path to the field of type `nested`.

`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.

type.description: The child type that should be selected.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

keyed.description:
By default, the aggregation associates a unique string key with each bucket and returns the ranges as a hash rather than an array.
Set to `false` to disable this behavior.

values.description: An array of values for which to calculate the percentile ranks.

hdr.description: Uses the alternative High Dynamic Range Histogram algorithm to calculate percentile ranks.

tdigest.description: Sets parameters for the default TDigest algorithm used to calculate percentile ranks.

number_of_significant_value_digits.description: Specifies the resolution of values for the histogram in number of significant digits.

compression.description: Limits the maximum number of nodes used by the underlying TDigest algorithm to `20 * compression`, enabling control of memory usage and approximation error.

execution_hint.description:
The default implementation of TDigest is optimized for performance, scaling to millions or even billions of sample values while maintaining acceptable accuracy levels (close to 1% relative error for millions of samples in some cases).
To use an implementation optimized for accuracy, set this parameter to high_accuracy instead.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

keyed.description:
By default, the aggregation associates a unique string key with each bucket and returns the ranges as a hash rather than an array.
Set to `false` to disable this behavior.

hdr.description: Uses the alternative High Dynamic Range Histogram algorithm to calculate percentiles.

tdigest.description: Sets parameters for the default TDigest algorithm used to calculate percentiles.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

percents.description: The list of percentiles to calculate.

field.description: The date field whose values are use to build ranges.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

ranges.description: An array of ranges used to bucket documents.

keyed.description: Set to `true` to associate a unique string key with each bucket and return the ranges as a hash rather than an array.

exclude.description: Terms that should be excluded from the aggregation.

field.description: The field from which to return rare terms.

include.description: Terms that should be included in the aggregation.

max_doc_count.description: The maximum number of documents a term should appear in.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

precision.description:
The precision of the internal CuckooFilters.
Smaller precision leads to better approximation, but higher memory usage.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

unit.description:
The interval used to calculate the rate.
By default, the interval of the `date_histogram` is used.

path.description:
Defines the nested object field that should be joined back to.
The default is empty, which means that it joins back to the root/main document level.

shard_size.description: Limits how many top-scoring documents are collected in the sample processed on each shard.

field.description: The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

combine_script.description:
Runs once on each shard after document collection is complete.
Allows the aggregation to consolidate the state returned from each shard.

init_script.description:
Runs prior to any collection of documents.
Allows the aggregation to set up any initial state.

map_script.description:
Run once per document collected.
If no `combine_script` is specified, the resulting state needs to be stored in the `state` object.

params.description:
A global object with script parameters for `init`, `map` and `combine` scripts.
It is shared between the scripts.

reduce_script.description:
Runs once on the coordinating node after all shards have returned their results.
The script is provided with access to a variable `states`, which is an array of the result of the `combine_script` on each shard.

buckets_path.description: Path to the buckets that contain one set of values to correlate.

format.description:
`DecimalFormat` pattern for the output value.
If specified, the formatted value is returned in the aggregation’s `value_as_string` property.

gap_policy.description: Policy to apply when gaps are found in the data.

lag.description:
The historical bucket to subtract from the current value.
Must be a positive, non-zero integer.

background_filter.description: A background filter that can be used to focus in on significant terms within a narrower context, instead of the entire index.

chi_square.description: Use Chi square, as described in "Information Retrieval", Manning et al., Chapter 13.5.2, as the significance score.

execution_hint.description: Mechanism by which the aggregation should be executed: using field values directly or using global ordinals.

field.description: The field from which to return significant terms.

gnd.description: Use Google normalized distance as described in "The Google Similarity Distance", Cilibrasi and Vitanyi, 2007, as the significance score.

jlh.description: Use JLH score as the significance score.

min_doc_count.description: Only return terms that are found in more than `min_doc_count` hits.

mutual_information.description: Use mutual information as described in "Information Retrieval", Manning et al., Chapter 13.5.1, as the significance score.

percentage.description: A simple calculation of the number of documents in the foreground sample with a term divided by the number of documents in the background with the term.

script_heuristic.description: Customized score, implemented via a script.

p_value.description:
Significant terms heuristic that calculates the p-value between the term existing in foreground and background sets.

The p-value is the probability of obtaining test results at least as extreme as
the results actually observed, under the assumption that the null hypothesis is
correct. The p-value is calculated assuming that the foreground set and the
background set are independent https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bernoulli_trial, with the null
hypothesis that the probabilities are the same.

shard_min_doc_count.description:
Regulates the certainty a shard has if the term should actually be added to the candidate list or not with respect to the `min_doc_count`.
Terms will only be considered if their local shard frequency within the set is higher than the `shard_min_doc_count`.

shard_size.description:
Can be used to control the volumes of candidate terms produced by each shard.
By default, `shard_size` will be automatically estimated based on the number of shards and the `size` parameter.

size.description: The number of buckets returned out of the overall terms list.

background_is_superset.description: Set to `false` if you defined a custom background filter that represents a different set of documents that you want to compare to.

include_negatives.description: Set to `false` to filter out the terms that appear less often in the subset than in documents outside the subset.

background_is_superset.description: Set to `false` if you defined a custom background filter that represents a different set of documents that you want to compare to.
Set to `false` if you defined a custom background filter that represents a different set of documents that you want to compare to.

include_negatives.description: Set to `false` to filter out the terms that appear less often in the subset than in documents outside the subset.

normalize_above.description:
Should the results be normalized when above the given value.
Allows for consistent significance results at various scales.
Note: `0` is a special value which means no normalization

filter_duplicate_text.description: Whether to out duplicate text to deal with noisy data.

jlh.description: Use JLH score as the significance score.

mutual_information.description: Use mutual information as described in "Information Retrieval", Manning et al., Chapter 13.5.1, as the significance score.

Values to include.

value_type.description: Coerced unmapped fields into the specified type.

order.description:

Defaults to sorting by descending document count.

Regulates the certainty a shard has if the term should actually be added to the candidate list or not with respect to the `min_doc_count`.

The maximum number of results to return.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

docvalue_fields.description: Fields for which to return doc values.

explain.description: If `true`, returns detailed information about score computation as part of a hit.

fields.description:
Array of wildcard (*) patterns. The request returns values for field names
matching these patterns in the hits.fields property of the response.

highlight.description: Specifies the highlighter to use for retrieving highlighted snippets from one or more fields in the search results.

script_fields.description: Returns the result of one or more script evaluations for each hit.

size.description: The maximum number of top matching hits to return per bucket.

sort.description:
Sort order of the top matching hits.
By default, the hits are sorted by the score of the main query.

_source.description: Selects the fields of the source that are returned.

stored_fields.description: Returns values for the specified stored fields (fields that use the `store` mapping option).

track_scores.description: If `true`, calculates and returns document scores, even if the scores are not used for sorting.

version.description: If `true`, returns document version as part of a hit.

seq_no_primary_term.description: If `true`, returns sequence number and primary term of the last modification of each hit.

field.description: The field to aggregate.

filter.description: A filter used to define a set of records to run unpaired t-test on.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

metrics.description: The fields of the top document to return.

size.description: The number of top documents from which to return metrics.

sort.description: The sort order of the documents.

field.description: A field to return as a metric.
The field on which to run the aggregation.

missing.description:
The value to apply to documents that do not have a value.
By default, documents without a value are ignored.

format.description: A numeric response formatter.

value.description: Configuration for the field that provides the values.

weight.description: Configuration for the field or script that provides the weights.

field.description: The field from which to extract the values or weights.

missing.description: A value or weight to use if the field is missing.

field.description: The name of the field.

shard_size.description:
The number of buckets that the coordinating node will request from each shard.
Defaults to `buckets * 50`.

initial_buffer.description:
Specifies the number of individual documents that will be stored in memory on a shard before the initial bucketing algorithm is run.
Defaults to `min(10 * shard_size, 50000)`.

value.description:
The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

unless specified otherwise.

unless specified otherwise.

_types.aggregations__SumAggregate.description: Sum aggregation result. `value` is always present and is zero if there were no values to process.

value.description:
The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

_types.aggregations__WeightedAvgAggregat.description: Weighted average aggregation result. `value` is missing if the weight was set to zero.

value.description:
The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

_types.aggregations__ValueCountAggregate.description: Value count aggregation result. `value` is always present.

value.description:
The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

The metric value. A missing value generally means that there was no data to aggregate,
unless specified otherwise.

_types.aggregations__StatsAggregate.description:
Statistics aggregation result. `min`, `max` and `avg` are missing if there were no values to process
(`count` is zero).

interval.description:
A date histogram interval. Similar to `Duration` with additional units: `w` (week), `M` (month), `q` (quarter) and
`y` (year)

_types.aggregations__StringTermsAggregat.description: Result of a `terms` aggregation when the field is a string.

_types.aggregations__LongTermsAggregate.description: Result of a `terms` aggregation when the field is some kind of whole number like a integer, long, or a date.

_types.aggregations__DoubleTermsAggregat.description: Result of a `terms` aggregation when the field is some kind of decimal number like a float, double, or distance.

_types.aggregations__UnmappedTermsAggreg.description: Result of a `terms` aggregation when the field is unmapped. `buckets` is always empty.

_types.aggregations__LongRareTermsAggreg.description: Result of the `rare_terms` aggregation when the field is some kind of whole number like a integer, long, or a date.

_types.aggregations__StringRareTermsAggr.description: Result of the `rare_terms` aggregation when the field is a string.

_types.aggregations__UnmappedRareTermsAg.description: Result of a `rare_terms` aggregation when the field is unmapped. `buckets` is always empty.

_types.aggregations__DateRangeAggregate.description:
Result of a `date_range` aggregation. Same format as a for a `range` aggregation: `from` and `to`
in `buckets` are milliseconds since the Epoch, represented as a floating point number.

_types.aggregations__GeoDistanceAggregat.description: Result of a `geo_distance` aggregation. The unit for `from` and `to` is meters by default.

_types.aggregations__UnmappedSignificant.description: Result of the `significant_terms` aggregation on an unmapped field. `buckets` is always empty.

name.description: Path to field or array of paths. Some API's support wildcards in the path to select multiple fields.
